---
title: "Hannah Szu-Han Wang"
subtitle: "PhD Candidate · Yale University"
about:
  template: solana
  image: images/avatar.jpg
  image-shape: round
  links:
    - icon: github
      text: GitHub
      href: https://github.com/SzuHannah
---

## Biography

I am an industrial ecologist with more than 5 years of experience in sustainable material design. My current research develops machine-learning-assisted sustainability assessment frameworks and indices that integrate economic and environmental impacts to guide sustainable biomaterial design, including biochar, biographite, and cross-laminated timber. At Yale, I work with [Dr. Yuan Yao](https://environment.yale.edu/directory/faculty/yuan-yao) and [Dr. Robert Mendelsohn](https://environment.yale.edu/directory/faculty/robert-mendelsohn).

I was fortunate to work with amazing mentors that inspired me to work at the intersection of ecosystem, engineering, decision-making, and computation. Before PhD, I was a data analyst in [Dr. Karl Rohe](https://pages.stat.wisc.edu/~karlrohe/)'s lab. During then, I applied network analysis to Bitcoin transactions and microbial communities. In my undergrad and master, I studied how environments and human behaviors interact. This includes biomaterial development with [Dr. Feng-cheng Chang](https://scholar.google.com/citations?user=6YSzB_YAAAAJ&hl=en), and social psychology research with [Dr. Ling Chen](https://scholar.google.com/citations?user=_o5Uv5wAAAAJ&hl=en).  

:::: {.columns}

::: {.column width="50%"}
::: {.details-section}
### Interests

- System engineering  
- Machine learning  
- Sustainable biomaterial design  
- Geospatial analysis  
- Uncertainty quantification  

:::
:::

::: {.column width="50%"}
::: {.details-section}
### Education

**PhD in Environment, exp.2026**  
Yale University

**MA in Statistics, 2024**  
Yale University

**MSc in Forestry-Biomaterial, 2020**  
National Taiwan University

**BSc in Forestry / Psychology, 2017**  
National Taiwan University
:::
:::

::::
